local_dishes.name: Ceviche
local_dishes.description: Peru's national dish, made with fresh raw fish or seafood 'cooked' in lime or lemon juice, mixed with chili, salt, red onions, sweet potato, and Peruvian corn
source_files: South America/Craig_ All Inclusive Guide - Peru.pdf
South America/All Inclusive Guide - Peru- Neha and Mayur.pdf
South America/All Inclusive Guide - Peru_CRE.pdf

local_dishes.name: Aji de Gallina
local_dishes.description: Creamy, comforting dish made with shredded chicken in rich, mild yellow sauce, served with rice, boiled potatoes, and black olives
source_files: South America/Craig_ All Inclusive Guide - Peru.pdf
South America/All Inclusive Guide - Peru- Neha and Mayur.pdf

local_dishes.name: Lomo Saltado

local_dishes.name: Causa Rellena

local_dishes.name: Papa a la Huancaína

local_dishes.name: Locro de Zapallo

local_dishes.name: Kapchi

local_dishes.name: Quinoa Soup

local_dishes.name: Tacu Tacu

local_dishes.name: Solterito

local_dishes.name: Humitas

local_dishes.name: Arroz Chaufa

local_dishes.name: Pisco Sour

safety_tips.tip: Be aware of your surroundings, especially in crowded areas like markets, bus terminals, and tourist sites
safety_tips._source_file: South America/Craig_ All Inclusive Guide - Peru.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Avoid walking alone at night, especially in less-trafficked or poorly lit areas
safety_tips._source_file: South America/Craig_ All Inclusive Guide - Peru.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Minimize displays of expensive jewelry, watches, cameras, or large amounts of cash
safety_tips._source_file: South America/Craig_ All Inclusive Guide - Peru.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Beware of pickpockets in crowded areas, keep bags zipped and in front of you
safety_tips._source_file: South America/Craig_ All Inclusive Guide - Peru.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Use ATMs during daylight hours, preferably inside banks or well-lit, secure locations
safety_tips._source_file: South America/Craig_ All Inclusive Guide - Peru.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Always use official, registered taxis or ride-sharing apps like Uber or Cabify
safety_tips._source_file: South America/Craig_ All Inclusive Guide - Peru.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Take it easy for first 24-48 hours in high altitude areas like Cusco to avoid altitude sickness
safety_tips._source_file: South America/Craig_ All Inclusive Guide - Peru.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Drink plenty of bottled water only, avoid tap water
safety_tips._source_file: South America/Craig_ All Inclusive Guide - Peru.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Be cautious with street food, choose clean establishments with high turnover
safety_tips._source_file: South America/Craig_ All Inclusive Guide - Peru.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Be wary of 'Good Samaritan' scams where strangers offer unsolicited help
safety_tips._source_file: South America/Craig_ All Inclusive Guide - Peru.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Be aware of fake police scams - always ask for official identification
safety_tips._source_file: South America/Craig_ All Inclusive Guide - Peru.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Negotiate taxi fares before getting in if not using meter or ride-sharing app
safety_tips._source_file: South America/Craig_ All Inclusive Guide - Peru.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Stay alert and pay attention to surroundings, especially in crowded areas like markets, bus terminals, and tourist sites
safety_tips._source_file: South America/All Inclusive Guide - Peru- Neha and Mayur.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Avoid walking alone at night, especially in less-trafficked or poorly lit areas. Take registered taxi or ride-sharing service
safety_tips._source_file: South America/All Inclusive Guide - Peru- Neha and Mayur.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Minimize displays of expensive jewelry, watches, cameras, or large amounts of cash
safety_tips._source_file: South America/All Inclusive Guide - Peru- Neha and Mayur.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Be careful of pickpockets in crowded areas. Keep bags zipped and in front of you, especially in markets like San Pedro Market
safety_tips._source_file: South America/All Inclusive Guide - Peru- Neha and Mayur.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Use ATMs during daylight hours, preferably inside banks or well-lit, secure locations
safety_tips._source_file: South America/All Inclusive Guide - Peru- Neha and Mayur.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Always use official, registered taxis or ride-sharing apps like Uber or Cabify when in Lima
safety_tips._source_file: South America/All Inclusive Guide - Peru- Neha and Mayur.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Don't drink tap water. Stick to bottled water to avoid stomach issues
safety_tips._source_file: South America/All Inclusive Guide - Peru- Neha and Mayur.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Take it easy for first 24-48 hours when heading to Cusco or Sacred Valley due to high altitudes. Avoid strenuous activity, heavy meals, and alcohol
safety_tips._source_file: South America/All Inclusive Guide - Peru- Neha and Mayur.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Drink plenty of bottled water and consider coca tea to help with altitude sickness
safety_tips._source_file: South America/All Inclusive Guide - Peru- Neha and Mayur.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Be cautious with street food; choose establishments that look clean and have high turnover
safety_tips._source_file: South America/All Inclusive Guide - Peru- Neha and Mayur.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Be wary of strangers offering unsolicited help, especially if they try to distract you
safety_tips._source_file: South America/All Inclusive Guide - Peru- Neha and Mayur.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Be aware of individuals posing as police officers. Always ask for official identification and never hand over passport or wallet
safety_tips._source_file: South America/All Inclusive Guide - Peru- Neha and Mayur.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Stay Alert: Pay attention to what's happening around you, especially in crowded areas like markets, bus terminals, and tourist sites
safety_tips._source_file: South America/All Inclusive Guide - Peru_CRE.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Avoid Walking Alone at Night: Especially in less-trafficked or poorly lit areas. Take a registered taxi or ride-sharing service
safety_tips._source_file: South America/All Inclusive Guide - Peru_CRE.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Minimize Displays: Avoid flashing expensive jewelry, watches, cameras, or large amounts of cash
safety_tips._source_file: South America/All Inclusive Guide - Peru_CRE.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Beware of Pickpockets: In crowded areas, keep bags zipped and in front of you. Be especially careful in markets (like San Pedro Market)
safety_tips._source_file: South America/All Inclusive Guide - Peru_CRE.pdf

safety_tips.tip: ATMs: Use ATMs during daylight hours, preferably inside banks or well-lit, secure locations. Be mindful of anyone watching you
safety_tips._source_file: South America/All Inclusive Guide - Peru_CRE.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Bags: When sitting at a cafe or restaurant, don't hang your bag on the back of your chair; keep it on your lap or between your feet
safety_tips._source_file: South America/All Inclusive Guide - Peru_CRE.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Registered Taxis: Always use official, registered taxis (they usually have a taxi sign on top and company numbers on the side) or ride-sharing apps like Uber or Cabify when in Lima
safety_tips._source_file: South America/All Inclusive Guide - Peru_CRE.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Hydration: Drink plenty of water (bottled water only!)
safety_tips._source_file: South America/All Inclusive Guide - Peru_CRE.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Food & Water: Stick to bottled water. Be cautious with street food; choose establishments that look clean and have high turnover. Wash fruits and vegetables thoroughly
safety_tips._source_file: South America/All Inclusive Guide - Peru_CRE.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Good Samaritan Scams: Be wary of strangers who approach you offering unsolicited help, especially if they try to distract you
safety_tips._source_file: South America/All Inclusive Guide - Peru_CRE.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Fake Police: There have been reports of individuals posing as police officers to demand money or search tourists' bags. Always ask for official identification and never hand over your passport or wallet. If suspicious, insist on going to the nearest police station
safety_tips._source_file: South America/All Inclusive Guide - Peru_CRE.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Overcharging: Negotiate taxi fares before getting in, especially if not using a meter or ride-sharing app. Check restaurant bills carefully
safety_tips._source_file: South America/All Inclusive Guide - Peru_CRE.pdf

health_tips.tip: Recommended vaccinations include: diphtheria, tetanus, polio, typhus, hepatitis A/B, and rabies
health_tips._source_file: South America/Craig_ All Inclusive Guide - Peru.pdf

health_tips.tip: Yellow fever vaccine strongly recommended for Amazon trips (Puerto Maldonado or Iquitos) and required for travelers from yellow fever-infected countries
health_tips._source_file: South America/Craig_ All Inclusive Guide - Peru.pdf

health_tips.tip: Yellow fever certificate valid 10 days after vaccination and remains valid for 10 years
health_tips._source_file: South America/Craig_ All Inclusive Guide - Peru.pdf

health_tips.tip: CDC recommends anti-malarial drugs if traveling to northern Peru and Iquitos in Amazon

health_tips.tip: Consult with your doctor for most up-to-date vaccination and anti-malarial medication advice

health_tips.tip: Carry prescriptions in original packaging plus digital copy

health_tips.tip: Recommended vaccinations include: diphtheria, tetanus, polio, typhus, hepatitis A/B, and rabies

health_tips.tip: Yellow fever certificate is valid 10 days after vaccination and remains valid for 10 years

health_tips.tip: Consult with your doctor for most up-to-date information and advice on vaccinations and anti-malarial medication
health_tips._source_file: South America/All Inclusive Guide - Peru- Neha and Mayur.pdf

health_tips._source_file: South America/All Inclusive Guide - Peru- Neha and Mayur.pdf

health_tips.tip: Recommended vaccinations for Peru include: diphtheria, tetanus, polio, typhus, hepatitis A/B, and rabies

A yellow fever vaccine is strongly recommended for trips to the Amazon (Puerto Maldonado or Iquitos) and required for travelers arriving from yellow fever-infected countries in Africa or Latin America
The yellow fever certificate is valid 10 days after vaccination and remains valid for a period of 10 years
The Center for Disease Control and Prevention recommends taking anti-malarial drugs if traveling to the northern parts of Peru and Iquitos in the Amazon
It is important to consult with your doctor for the most up-to-date information and advice on vaccinations and anti-malarial medication for your specific travel plans in Peru
Carry prescriptions in original packaging + digital copy
